articles.title: STORYTELLING: AN AI ERA SURVIVAL SKILL FOR PRODUCT MANAGERS
articles.summary: AI has commoditized typical PM artifacts like research summaries and specs, so evidence alone no longer persuades stakeholders. The PMs who stand out will be those who transform real customer insight into clear, audience-specific stories that build trust and drive decisions.

articles.title: WHAT'S HAPPENING IN OPENAI'S AGENTIC COMMERCE
articles.summary: OpenAI ended its Instant Checkout experiment in ChatGPT because it conflicted with its larger strategy around ads and the app ecosystem, not because agentic commerce failed. The broader trend continues as companies like Shopify and Google build infrastructure for AI-driven purchasing through merchant-owned apps and protocols.

articles.title: CAPACITY IS THE ROADMAP
articles.summary: Roadmaps are determined by team capacity, not by the number of ideas competing for priority. Good planning shapes work before prioritizing it and allocates limited time deliberately, making trade-offs and displacement explicit.

articles.title: EVERY COMPANY IS A STARTUP NOW
articles.summary: AI has lowered the cost of building software, allowing small teams to challenge incumbents that once relied on scale and process for protection. Companies that rewarded planning over shipping now struggle to compete with faster, builder-focused startups.

articles.title: HOW PRODUCT LEADERS ARE SHIPPING AI AGENTS IN WEEKS, NOT QUARTERS
articles.summary: Custom integrations are slow, expensive, and brittle. ThoughtSpot's MCP framework lets you plug real-time, governed analytics directly into your AI agents, so your product gets smarter without your engineering team getting buried. Download the MCP playbook for product

articles.title: MY PREFERRED PRODUCT MANAGEMENT TECHNIQUES AND FRAMEWORKS
articles.summary: Strong product management uses clear frameworks for discovery, strategy, vision, roadmapping, alignment, and measurement. Methods like direct observation, Now-Next-Later roadmaps, and outcome-focused metrics help teams understand problems, prioritize effectively, and move toward a clear product vision.

articles.title: UX ROUNDUP: OLD VS NEW UX | ADAM SMITH PREDICTS AI | AI VIDEO
articles.summary: Most UX guidance is still stuck in the pre-AI era, even though the future of design is increasingly shaped by AI-driven workflows, generative interfaces, and agent-based experiences. AI will reshape the economy, commerce, and media by making intelligence cheaper, commerce more autonomous, and content creation far more scalable.

articles.title: BE HONEST IF GROWTH HAS SLOWED. IS YOUR PRODUCT STILL MAGICAL? IT PROBABLY … USED TO BE
articles.summary: Many SaaS products that once felt magical have become stale, and AI is raising the bar for what software must deliver. Companies must either reinvent their products with AI-native capabilities or accept slower growth and eventual decline.

articles.title: WHEN BUILDING BECOMES CHEAPER THAN SPECIFYING, EVERYTHING CHANGES
articles.summary: AI has made building software prototypes far cheaper and faster, allowing teams to test ideas with real products instead of long specifications. As a result, organizations that build to learn quickly will innovate faster than those still optimized for planning before building.

articles.title: ARE THEY SOFTWARE COMPANIES OR DO THEY JUST HAVE SOFTWARE PROBLEMS?
articles.summary: Many companies thought of as software companies actually use software only to distribute their real value, such as content, services, or networks.

articles.title: 143 MILLION POKÉMON PLAYERS ACCIDENTALLY BUILT A MASSIVE AI DATASET
articles.summary: Pokémon GO players unknowingly generated a dataset of 30+ billion real-world images through AR scans during gameplay.
